title: Is Strait of Hormuz Open Again? Maybe, but Few Ships Are Using It.

title: Has Trump Met His War Goals in Iran?

title: White House Knew About Pakistan’s Cease-Fire Post on X Before It Was Sent
summary: Pakistan’s prime minister posted a public plea on X for President Trump to extend his Tuesday evening deadline for Iran. The White House was directly involved in shaping the message.

title: Gilgo Beach Killer’s ‘Unusual’ Deal Could Help Solve Other Murders
summary: Rex Heuermann agreed to talk to behavioral analysts at the F.B.I. when he pleaded guilty. The unit has interviewed Ted Bundy, John Wayne Gacy and Richard Speck.

title: In 20 Minutes, Gilgo Beach Killer Admits to Murdering 8 Women
summary: As he pleaded guilty on Wednesday, Rex Heuermann gave the same one-word answer each time he was asked how he killed one of his victims.

title: White House Secures Foreign Steel for Trump’s Ballroom Project
summary: ArcelorMittal, a European steel maker, is donating tens of millions of dollars of foreign steel for President Trump’s new ballroom.

title: Bondi Won’t Appear on Capitol Hill for Scheduled Epstein Deposition
summary: Pam Bondi had already been working to avoid testifying before she was fired as attorney general. The House Oversight Committee said she would not honor her subpoena because she was no longer in the post.

title: Federal Court Denies Anthropic’s Motion to Lift ‘Supply Chain Risk’ Label

title: Trump Administration Investigating L.A. Schools’ Gender Disclosure Policies
summary: The investigation into the nation’s second-largest school district was prompted by a lawsuit from parents who say the policies contributed to their child’s death.

title: On Truth Social, Trump Supporters Fume About Iran War
summary: A growing chorus of disaffected Trump supporters is sounding off in the replies to his posts on the social media platform he founded.

title: Blue Collar Work Has Plateaued, Narrowing Options for Young Workers
summary: Skilled electricians, plumbers and factory workers are in demand, but job openings have dropped.

title: ‘Ketamine Queen’ Sentenced to 15 Years in Matthew Perry’s Overdose Death
summary: The sentence for Jasveen Sangha, who prosecutors say was known to customers as the Ketamine Queen, is the stiffest yet for those charged in the “Friends” star’s death.

title: Hasan Piker Can Stream, but Can He Stump?
summary: The progressive and popular Twitch streamer, who is controversial with some Democrats, tests his political influence on the 2026 campaign trail.

summary: A lot of the money flowing into the political system is ultimately untraceable.

title: Patients Are Using Chatbots to Fight Medical Bills, With Mixed Results
summary: While chatbots like Claude and ChatGPT can help narrow the information divide between patients and providers, they can also dispense flawed advice.

title: Meta Unveils New A.I. Model, Its First From the Superintelligence Lab
summary: The model, Muse Spark, performed better than Meta’s previous A.I. models but lags rivals on coding ability.

title: An Audacious $724 Million Building Reinvents LACMA
summary: Two decades in the making, the David Geffen Galleries will offer an unconventional approach to art history and cement the director Michael Govan’s legacy.

title: Jim Whittaker, First American to Reach Everest’s Summit, Dies at 97
summary: As an executive with the outdoor-supply retailer REI and an experienced climber, he conquered Mount Everest in 1963, when fewer than 10 people were known to have done so.

title: California Supreme Court Orders Sheriff to Halt Election Investigation
summary: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a candidate for governor, had seized ballots from a 2025 special election based on unsubstantiated claims of election irregularities.

title: Wayne Perkins, Guitarist to the Stars, Dies at 74
summary: Mr. Perkins worked with Bob Marley, Joni Mitchell and many others, almost joined the Rolling Stones and turned down an offer from Lynyrd Skynyrd.

title: Parts of Hawaii Face a Foot or More of Rain From Yet Another Storm
summary: Last month was one of the wettest Marches on record for the state, leading to widespread flooding and evacuations. More rain is hitting this week.
